introSection: GhostRider is a Custom Coasters International wooden roller coaster located at Knott's Berry Farm in Buena Park, California, USA. Opened in 1998, the ride has a double out and back pattern.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Testing began on November 20, 1998. GhostRider opened on December 8, 1998.

On September 8, 2015, GhostRider was closed for a complete retracking by Great Coasters International. The three Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters trains were replaced with Millennium Flyers. The ride was originally planned to reopen on May 27, 2016, but this was delayed. It reopened on June 11.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains are painted gold, silver, and copper. Although the coaster has three trains, it operates two trains at a time.

fullText:
GhostRider is a Custom Coasters International wooden roller coaster located at Knott's Berry Farm in Buena Park, California, USA. Opened in 1998, the ride has a double out and back pattern.

Testing began on November 20, 1998. GhostRider opened on December 8, 1998.

On September 8, 2015, GhostRider was closed for a complete retracking by Great Coasters International. The three Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters trains were replaced with Millennium Flyers. The ride was originally planned to reopen on May 27, 2016, but this was delayed. It reopened on June 11.



The trains are painted gold, silver, and copper. Although the coaster has three trains, it operates two trains at a time.
